title: lavandula pedunculata subsp. sampaioana
intro: Lavandula pedunculata subsp. sampaioana, also known as Sampaio's lavender, is a subspecies of lavender native to Portugal. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils, particularly camphor, linalool, and linalyl acetate. The health benefits of Lavandula pedunculata subsp. sampaioana include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, essential oils, and topical creams, which can be used to promote relaxation, relieve pain, and support overall well-being.
